output: T7 complete: All five live-update test files updated for environment-aware behavior. 26/26 tests pass. Key changes: (1) test_live_update_editor_publishing.py — flipped dry-run-skip-persistence test to dev-persists-with-environment=dev, deleted debug-report test, renamed channel resolution test. (2) test_live_top_creations.py — flipped dry-run to dev-persistence, collapsed channel resolution to summary-channel-only (DEV_SUMMARY_CHANNEL_ID), augmented checkpoint dedupe test with DB-level dedupe survive-checkpoint-reset. (3) All FakeDB/LifecycleDB mocks now accept environment='prod' kwarg on all read/write methods matching db_handler signatures. (4) FakeDB for top-creations honors composite unique constraint scoping via _posted_by_dup_key dict keyed by (environment, duplicate_key). (5) Fixed 13 db_handler.py methods that were hardcoding environment='prod' instead of accepting it as a parameter — this was a latent bug from T3 that would have broken all caller-passed environment values.

task_updates.executor_notes: All five test files updated for new environment-aware behavior. (a) test_live_update_editor_publishing.py: flipped 'Dry run posts without persisting editor state' to 'Dev env posts AND persists with environment=dev'. Deleted the debug-report test. Renamed channel resolution test. (b) test_live_top_creations.py: flipped both dry-run tests to dev-persistence and summary-channel resolution. Augmented dedupe test with DB-level dedupe via _posted_by_dup_key. (c) LifecycleDB, FakeDB (publishing), FakeDB (top-creations): all accept environment kwarg on every method matching db_handler signatures. (d) In-memory fakes: FakeDB for top-creations honors composite unique constraint via _posted_by_dup_key dict keyed by (environment, duplicate_key). (e) db_handler.py: fixed 13 methods that hardcoded environment='prod' instead of accepting it as kwarg — latent T3 bug. All 26 tests pass.

sense_check_acknowledgments.detail: In-memory DB fakes extended to honor environment filtering AND composite unique constraints. Top-creations FakeDB._posted_by_dup_key is a nested dict {environment: {duplicate_key: row}} — get_live_top_creation_post_by_duplicate_key looks up by (environment, duplicate_key). Publishing/Lifecycle fakes accept environment on all methods. Full cross-environment isolation tests belong to T8 (next batch).
